org.arakhne.neteditor.fsm.figures

Class FSMFigureFactory

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.factory.AbstractFigureFactory<G>
      • org.arakhne.neteditor.fig.factory.AbstractStandardFigureFactory<FiniteStateMachine>
        • org.arakhne.neteditor.fsm.figures.FSMFigureFactory
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    FigureFactory<FiniteStateMachine>

    public class FSMFigureFactory
extends AbstractStandardFigureFactory<FiniteStateMachine>
    Factory for figures of a FSM.
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:03:12
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor.fsm
    Maven Artifact Id:
    fsm-figures

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      Figure createFigureFor(UUID viewID, FiniteStateMachine graph, ModelObject object, float x, float y)
      Create a node figure for the given object at the specified position.
      Figure createFigureFor(UUID viewID, FiniteStateMachine graph, ModelObject object, float x1, float y1, float x2, float y2)
      Create an edge figure for the given object between the specified points.
      SubFigure createSubFigureInside(UUID viewID, FiniteStateMachine graph, Figure parent, ModelObject object)
      Create a sub figure for the given model object inside the given figure.
      protected Dimension getPreferredNodeSize(Node<?,?,?,?> node)
      Replies the preferred size for the specified node.

    • Constructor Detail

      • FSMFigureFactory

        public FSMFigureFactory()

    • Method Detail

      • createFigureFor

        public Figure createFigureFor(UUID viewID,
                     FiniteStateMachine graph,
                     ModelObject object,
                     float x,
                     float y)
                       throws FigureFactoryException
        Create a node figure for the given object at the specified position.
        Parameters:
        viewID - is the identifier of the view.
        graph - is the graph inside which the model was added.
        object - is the model object for which a view should be created.
        x - is the position of the new set of figures.
        y - is the position of the new set of figures.
        Returns:
        the created view.
        Throws:
        FigureFactoryException

      • createSubFigureInside

        public SubFigure createSubFigureInside(UUID viewID,
                              FiniteStateMachine graph,
                              Figure parent,
                              ModelObject object)
        Create a sub figure for the given model object inside the given figure.
        Parameters:
        viewID - is the identifier of the view.
        graph - is the graph inside which the model was added.
        parent - is the figure inside which the subfigure may be inserted.
        object - is the model object for which a view should be created.
        Returns:
        the created view.

      • createFigureFor

        public Figure createFigureFor(UUID viewID,
                     FiniteStateMachine graph,
                     ModelObject object,
                     float x1,
                     float y1,
                     float x2,
                     float y2)
                       throws FigureFactoryException
        Create an edge figure for the given object between the specified points.
        Parameters:
        viewID - is the identifier of the view.
        graph - is the graph inside which the model was added.
        object - is the model object for which a view should be created.
        x1 - is the position of the first point of the figure.
        y1 - is the position of the first point of the figure.
        x2 - is the position of the second point of the figure.
        y2 - is the position of the second point of the figure.
        Returns:
        the created view.
        Throws:
        FigureFactoryException

      • getPreferredNodeSize

        protected Dimension getPreferredNodeSize(Node<?,?,?,?> node)
        Description copied from class: AbstractFigureFactory
        Replies the preferred size for the specified node.
        Specified by:
        getPreferredNodeSize in class AbstractFigureFactory<FiniteStateMachine>
        Returns:
        the size, never null.
